title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly is participating in the 2026 Boston Marathon for her former patient, Giovanni Thompson.
Giovanni Thompson, a young patient with Morquio syndrome, inspired Donnelly through his determination and spirit.
Donnelly remembers Gio for his charming personality and resilience, as well as the impact he had on her life.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: New Jersey's Paul Cotter scored the winning goal in overtime, clinching a 4-3 victory over the Boston Bruins.
Jack Hughes assisted three goals, reaching 402 career points, including on Cotter's overtime goal.
David Pastrnak scored twice for Boston, but the Bruins remain second in the wild-card race with 81 points.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, charged with murder after remains found under garage floor, likely missing Lowell woman Jill Kloppenburg.
The remains were found in a cement-sealed bag after police scanned and excavated the garage floor, says DA Ryan.
Sullivan allegedly confessed to a friend in Nashua, NH, that he shot and buried Kloppenburg, missing since January 2025.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise money for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman's son, Charles, has Dup15q syndrome, requiring specialized care from MGH doctor Ronald Thibert.
Dr. Thibert has provided 14 years of dedicated care, improving Charles's quality of life amidst ongoing seizures.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his wife, Coney Flowers.
Coney Flowers battled Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia and underwent a successful bone marrow transplant in 2024.
Ben is raising funds for Caring for a Cure, an organization that supported them during Coney's treatment.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's Pediatric Cancer team.
Labrie's participation is part of the "I Run For Them" initiative, honoring children affected by cancer and other diseases.
Donors can contribute in honor of specific individuals, adding to the increasing "I Run For Them" community.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary 'Noah Kahan: Out of Body' premieres on Netflix April 13.
Kahan's documentary covers his breakout success, mental health journey, and Vermont roots.
His fourth studio album, 'The Great Divide,' releases on April 24, shortly after the documentary.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ-TV as a 'special contributor' after layoffs last fall.
Keller's 'Keller At Large' returns Sunday at 8:30 a.m. with guest Gov. Maura Healey.
Keller will continue his MASSterList column and contribute to Boston magazine.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports a bill to end Massachusetts' biannual clock change.
83% of over 470 Boston.com readers favor stopping the clock change; opinions vary on the permanent time system.
Concerns include health risks from clock changes and potential darker winter mornings if Atlantic time is adopted.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham drove 118 mph and led a police chase before crashing in New Hampshire.
The teenager failed to stop for police in both Maine and New Hampshire before losing control on Route 101.
Charged with multiple offenses, including DUI and reckless conduct, he was held at Rockingham County Jail.
